name: Julius Baker
abstract: Julius Baker (September 23, 1915 – August 6, 2003) was one of the foremost American orchestral flute players. During the course of five decades he concertized with several of America's premier orchestral ensembles including the Chicago Symphony and the New York Philharmonic Orchestra.

about_quotes.speaker: Hubert Laws
about_quotes.evidence: My previous teacher, Julius Baker... I’d go to my lessons with Julius Baker every weekend... I’m still glad he made us do it... that music is responsible for it, developing the memory.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hubert Laws — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Lou Marini
about_quotes.evidence: Rick Hickman, the oboe player, told me that he had a colleague in the college he was teaching in in New Jersey who was a student of Julius Baker’s
about_quotes.doc_title: Lou Marini interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 15, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Harold Mabern
about_quotes.evidence: Jesus, Julius Baker I think was principal flautist with the Philharmonic.
about_quotes.doc_title: Harold Mabern interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, March 31, 2010

about_quotes.speaker: Lew Tabackin
about_quotes.evidence: I didn’t study with Julius Baker, I listened to Julius Baker records.
about_quotes.doc_title: Lew Tabackin interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 17, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Buddy Collette
about_quotes.evidence: And I also heard Julius Baker and he did something of Alec Wilder’s.
about_quotes.doc_title: Buddy Collette interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 13, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Kent Jordan
about_quotes.evidence: he had the training in Juilliard and Julius Baker and all that stuff
about_quotes.doc_title: Kent Jordan interviewed by Monk Rowe, Rome, New York, August 22, 2006
